Ihe course as outlined will include instruction the
evolution of womanknd of the woong with rocks in the
Stone of the chae days of the Middle ages of her
present condition in most states where, equipped with
learning fnsh brains, integrity and conscience she is
denied a vote


The idea fine 1t should be broadened the course wild
ened, with every woman instructor.


The greatest crusaders to the real cause of woman
rights will not be found in the school room.


They will be found in the kitchens of humble homes
haired perhaps and with faces that show the marks
OF loving sacrifice They will be found grooming love
cradles and instillng into baby minds so strongly
and so sweety that it will be forgotten, the
great fact that in the Divine scheme of affairs woman
the greatest OF Gods creations to be denied nothing that
she wishes whether it be votes just love.


They will be found perhaps, taking up the burdens of
life where some weak brutal husband has left them
They will be found too in the sisters who teach their
boisterous brothers that the genteness of s heart
greater than the of the most lion hearted and
more powerful for good than the most ponderous phrases
from pulpit.


They will be found in the sweethearts who will lead their
sutors to the high realms of ideals and teach the great
truth that between there is dfferenc in mOras
who will demand from their husbands that same standard
OF virtue which is asked of them.


With such of training, the suffrage question
would be solved.


When the babe who grows to manhood with the song of
the young mother ringing in his memory when the youth
bearded but harks back the DAYS when his mother
patiently patched his trousers and breathed prayer that
he grow up into "gOod man, when the brother burn
shes the glass of memory and the sweetaced smiling
sister; when the husband thanks his God that HE
able to to the high standards of righteousness
demanded by the sweetaced woman who became his bride
when these come to the real objects of the suf
fragets will have been


For when these happen there will be faces in
sweatshops children sent out to labor before their fra
bodies have taken strength their minds equip
for the future crowding of girls into unheathfu
occupations, no debauchery by brutal employers.


Perhaps the school for suffrage alone bring these
results With the womanhood of America giving these
subsidiary courses it is bound to.
